note: Day 30 (Monday). Zero active connection notes. No Batch 5 Ryedale data — no new leads. All Batch 1-4 sequences complete since 2026-04-24. Warm pipeline leads 1/3/22 (Tom/Claire/Rob): final close emails assumed sent 2026-04-25 (Friday). Now Day 2 of 7-day buffer. LinkedIn nurture notes pre-generated and ready; earliest dispatch 2026-05-02 (Saturday), preferred 2026-05-04 (Monday). Post-breakup contingency notes for leads 50/52/55/56 carry forward — connection requests sent status remains UNCONFIRMED after 10 consecutive BRAIN runs without linkedin_profiles.json.

strategy.tone: slightly awkward genuine compliment — stands out from polished AI outreach slop
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by'
Do not mention AI or automation
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity only
Do not use 'reach out' or 'touch base'
Do not mention 'digital marketing' or 'web design' explicitly
Do not reference the email outreach in warm pipeline nurture notes
Do not reference any previous contact in warm pipeline nurture notes — treat as first contact
strategy.priority_upgrade_rule: HANDS: always check LinkedIn profile for posts from last 60 days BEFORE sending any note. If post found: use post_reference note (3-4x higher acceptance rate). If no recent posts: use generic note. This was NOT executed across 10 consecutive BRAIN runs in Batch 1-4. MANDATORY for Batch 5 and warm pipeline.
strategy.execution_protocol_batch_5: HANDS must open LinkedIn, search, check for posts, and send the note in a SINGLE SESSION on Day 3, 4, 5, or 6 after initial email. Do not split search and send across days. The window closes automatically on Day 7 when the follow-up email fires.
strategy.warm_pipeline_note_rule: Warm pipeline notes (leads 1/3/22) must NOT reference the email sequence, the campaign, or any prior contact. Treat as a cold LinkedIn connection from a new prospect who noticed their business online.

no_active_notes_reason: No leads in Day 3-6 LinkedIn eligibility window. All Batch 1-4 complete. No Batch 5 data. Warm pipeline 1/3/22 in 7-day post-close buffer (Day 2 of 7 — 5 days remaining).

warm_pipeline_nurture_notes.do_not_send_if: Any of these leads reply to the final close email at any point — even a rejection. LinkedIn is permanently closed if an email reply arrives.
warm_pipeline_nurture_notes.hands_check_required: Before sending any of these notes: HANDS must (1) confirm final close was sent 2026-04-25, (2) check IMAP on send day — if reply found after close was sent: cancel LinkedIn note for that lead immediately. (3) IMAP gap is 23 days — a silent reply is plausible for any of these leads.

notes.note: Tom — Ripon Road Carpentry came up when I was looking at trades businesses around Ripon. Something about the online presence caught my attention. Connecting in case it's worth a conversation.
notes.post_reference_upgrade: If Tom has posted about carpentry projects, heritage joinery, North Yorkshire builds, or trade work in the last 60 days: upgrade to post_reference. Keep under 200 chars.
notes.post_reference_template: Tom — saw your post about [X]. Ripon Road Carpentry came up when I was looking at trades businesses around Ripon. Something about the online presence caught my eye. Connecting in case useful.
notes.do_not_send_if: Tom has replied to the final close email at any point (including rejection)
notes.notes_about_lead: First-name-only lead. Spring carpentry season. Confirm the LinkedIn profile is clearly the owner/operator of Ripon Road Carpentry in Ripon before connecting. Email reply received 2026-04-04 — INTERESTED.

notes.note: Claire — Westfield Hair & Beauty came up when I was looking at independent salons in Leeds. Noticed something about the online presence worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.
notes.post_reference_upgrade: If Claire has posted about salon news, team achievements, client transformations, or Leeds business content in the last 60 days: upgrade to post_reference.
notes.post_reference_template: Claire — saw your post about [X]. Westfield Hair & Beauty caught my attention looking at independent salons in Leeds. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's relevant.
notes.do_not_send_if: Claire has replied to the final close email at any point. She booked a call (2026-04-07) — LinkedIn permanently blocked if email conversation resumes.
notes.notes_about_lead: STRONGEST warm pipeline lead. Booked a call, identified booking pain. High LinkedIn presence expected — salons typically active on social. Upgrade to post_reference if profile has recent posts. Do not reference the call or any previous contact.

notes.linkedin_search_fallback: site:linkedin.com/in print design owner director "Sowerby Bridge" OR "Halifax" "West Yorkshire"
notes.note: Rob — Pennine Print & Design stood out when I was looking at print businesses in Sowerby Bridge. Noticed something about the website worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's relevant.

notes.post_reference_template: Rob — saw your post about [X]. Pennine Print & Design came up looking at print businesses around Sowerby Bridge. Something about the website caught my attention. Connecting in case it's useful.
notes.do_not_send_if: Rob has replied to the final close email at any point. Upsell signal: 'sort the whole thing in one go' — highest revenue potential in campaign. If email conversation resumes: LinkedIn permanently blocked.
notes.notes_about_lead: HIGHEST REVENUE POTENTIAL — upsell signal. B2B print/design sector: high LinkedIn presence expected. Confirm profile ownership before connecting. Do not reference the email or any prior contact.

batch_5_note_templates.status: READY — templates prepared. Note generation begins on Day 3 after Batch 5 initial emails fire. If sends fire today (Monday 2026-04-27): Day 3 = Thursday 2026-05-01.
batch_5_note_templates.post_reference_template: [Name] — saw your post about [X]. [Business] caught my eye looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the site worth mentioning. Connecting in case it's useful.
batch_5_note_templates.generic_template: [Name] — [Business] came up when I was looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Something about the [website/online presence] caught my attention. Connecting in case it's relevant.
batch_5_note_templates.headline_template: [Name] — [headline reference] caught my attention. [Business] came up looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful.
batch_5_note_templates.char_limit_reminder: All notes must be trimmed to 200 chars or fewer before sending. Count carefully — LinkedIn free account limit is absolute.

upgrade_decision_matrix.post_from_last_60_days_found: USE post_reference — highest acceptance rate (3-4x over generic)
upgrade_decision_matrix.distinctive_headline_found: USE headline — e.g. 'Malton's only RICS-registered surveyor' → reference the credential specifically
upgrade_decision_matrix.active_profile_no_recent_post: USE generic — still effective, clean, non-AI-sounding
upgrade_decision_matrix.inactive_profile_or_no_profile: SKIP — log in linkedin_profiles.json as 'not_found' or 'inactive'. Do not send generic to a profile with zero activity.

sector_priority_batch_5.note: Ryedale target categories include joiners, builders, plumbers — expect lower LinkedIn presence for these trades. Prioritise SSL leads in high-LinkedIn sectors first.

campaign_linkedin_assessment.root_cause: HANDS execution latency. LinkedIn window (Day 3-6) is narrow and cannot be recovered once missed. Notes were generated correctly by BRAIN on 7 consecutive runs. Zero notes were confirmed sent by HANDS.
campaign_linkedin_assessment.key_learning: The 'post_reference upgrade' step requires HANDS to check each profile BEFORE sending — this step was not executed once across 30 campaign days. For Batch 5: HANDS must open LinkedIn, search, check for posts, and send in a SINGLE session on the correct day.
campaign_linkedin_assessment.batch_5_protocol_change: BRAIN will flag Day 3 LinkedIn send day explicitly in daily_signals.json as a MANDATORY action item with a 2-hour execution target. HANDS must write linkedin_profiles.json after every search session — even if no note was sent.
